title: EdhocInitiator and EdhocResponder expose private key material in Debug
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs

The EdhocInitiator and EdhocResponder structs do not implement Debug, but their internal state structs (InitiatorState, ResponderState) contain sensitive PRK values.

Looking at lines 197-225 (InitiatorState) and 617-646 (ResponderState), these contain:
- prk_2e, prk_3e2m, prk_4e3m (all 32-byte secrets)

While these structs are private and derive Zeroize, they don't prevent debug printing in certain scenarios. Compare to Context (lib.rs:190-206) which explicitly redacts secrets in its Debug impl.

If logging/debugging inadvertently captures these structs, key material could leak.

Impact: Potential secret leakage in logs or debug output.

Fix: Either add #[derive(Debug)] with custom impl that redacts secrets, or explicitly opt out with:
```rust
impl core::fmt::Debug for InitiatorState {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut core::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> core::fmt::Result {
        f.debug_struct("InitiatorState")
            .field("prk_2e", &"[REDACTED]")
            // ...
            .finish()
    }
}
```
